review_title: One System For All Data Needs
description: EDGE is an easy to use system can be used to house all your data. I use it more and more each day and find more and more uses for EDGE within our department. Great system and would recommend it as a data management system for research teams.
author: Pardeep J.
pros: The system is intuitive and easy to use with an easy to navigate site and simple but effective easy to follow GUI The system is very flexible in that it can be used to accommodate all data needs for any department. This is captured in the form of Entities or Workflows All the information you input can be made public or private depending on who you wish to view this data All the information can be reported on and easily exported to other programs such as Excel. This data can then be fed in to visualisation tools to allow quick and easy access to data that is easy to understand. The search facility used to create reports can save search criteria so continuous reports can be saved and run with ease daily/weekly/monthly. (would be great if this report could be scheduled to run automatically maybe in the next iteration of EDGE) Allows for standardisation of process through workflows and this can help standardise process trust wide or even further reaching. We have used this to help standardise particular processes across the region Can be used as document storage for clinical trials There is a global library available to anyone with EDGE access and this can be used to upload / download workflows and entities. Anything available here can be downloaded and tailored to suit the needs of the individual. This is a great idea and brings to the forefront the ideology of one NHS promoting best practice through sharing best practice. Support team are absolutely great
cons: No drag and drop option available for documents When adding workflows and entities greater customisation would be great e.g editing entity and workflow titles when adding to allow more than one of the same group of attributes to be added

review_title: EDGE - Much more than a research database
description: EDGE can create and support service change to help improve health research. It encourages innovation and collaboration amongst the research community and its development is very much driven by the users. It is very adaptable and easy to use.
author: Kate P.
pros: The system allows the admin-site to design the way the site uses the software. Each Research project is displayed with the same information however the site specific page can be tailor made to collect additional information about the project and has options to create a workflow to evidence a procedure or process. The system is much more than a research database where recruitment data is entered. EDGE adopts standard methodologies and processes for reporting and collecting business intelligence. It can be used for in-depth quality assurance checks, financial oversight as well as clinical oversight.
cons: The implementation of EDGE was made easy by the how adaptable the system is. Workflows have mostly replaced our forms and the electronic pathways have improved our performance as the tasks are completed in a timely fashion. There are some areas where we would prefer tailor-made restricted access. Sites have the ability to create a research project page and for Sponsors this is an advantage as the quality checks that occur from sponsorship application through to submission can be captured. The downside is that the research project is also entered onto the system by the national team which causes duplicate entries. This is a hindrance for sites that want to use EDGE for more than just a performance tool

description: Overall experience of edge is positive
author: Anthony H.
pros: Generally everything is quite customizable - this is good so we can record a lot of local information.
cons: Traceability and auditability needs a lot of work to be more usable - the level of interpretation required is quite high, its not really suitable for an NHS system to be so hard to track what people are looking at. Also edge could provide training videos or leaflets for us to talk new staff through rather than us localizing something 100 times over. The finance and workflow areas need to be more customizable like the attribute systems are. There should be ways of "deactivating" everything from any list of options without deleting, as the more data you add the messier the system gets if you cant limit the options at certain times or with certain studies.

description: I have used EDGE for 6+ years and am constantly finding new ways to use EDGE! Reports are run to manage our trial set up and amendment processes so we can keep track of where a study is in the pipeline and what tasks are needed next. We are able to prioritise studies in set up to ensure those that are most important to open get managed through the process first. We manage all patient activity through EDGE to not only review accrual but also to identify patient activity which can be billed. We use it to measure performance within each team comparing activity from one time period to another. We use it to check the quality of our data to ensure that we are not duplicating patients or inputting incorrect information relating to patients into the system.
author: Pia F.
pros: It is able to be individualised to an organisations needs. How much an organisation gets out of it is limited only by the limits of the organisation's imagination and its willingness to experiment. Information is available instantly. Reports can be generated to meet a whole range of stakeholder needs.
cons: It has some limitations in being able to generate reports that combine patient data with study set up data. We have managed to find a workaround to this which suits our needs, but it would be nice to see in future versions a way to run a report that combined project attributes with patient attributes to be able to query the data in different ways. There are limitations with using it as a financial management tool for trials and it would be good to see in future versions a way to upload information direct from the contract and to be able to generate invoices in this way. This would definitely be on my EDGE wish list!

description: Very positive. The team are great! Know their stuff and are open to suggestions / ideas.
author: Carolyn M.
pros: EDGE is NOT a Database. It is a very powerful tool limited only by your imagination or drive. EDGE allows us to know in real time exactly where we are with each study active within the organization. From Expression of Interest right the way through to Closedown and confirmation that a publication has been submitted. Every process for confirming capacity and capability across the organization including within Support Departments and other Governance aspects have access to EDGE. We are able to confirm that processes have been followed in accordance with our extensive range of SOPs by following our bespoke workflows. Study C&C can be followed through from beginning to end. Everyone can see where things are and take appropriate action. Stops multiple duplicate phone calls. I could wax lyrical about EDGE all day if you were to allow me to do so. Research Management has never been so easy! Transparent and complete! It also helps to know that if my addled brain forgets a process step, EDGE will remind me to do it because I've built it that way! It takes the intuition, guesswork and assumption out and replaces it with confirmation of process and positive progression.
cons: Some of the functionality is long winded but there are upgrades in the pipeline. Drag and drop, adding investigators, adding more than one Attribute / workflow at a time.

review_title: Integrated System for Governance and Reporting
description: Our experience with Edge is excellent. We can produce timely reports on all aspects of study set-up and management. Particularly helpful with DoH reporting for PII and PID. The system is flexible and enables fully integrated governance and oversight. We have extended the system for the studies for which we act as Sponsor and continue to customise the system. We receive excellent support from the Southampton Team.
author: Maria M.
pros: The system is accessible, easy to use and easy to customise according to our needs. We use Edge as a fully integrated governance system for studies for which we act as Sponsor and also as a participating research site. The system is used for basic recruitment information. We customised it to report and store information on all aspects of study set-up, end to end from site selection to contract and then for amendments. The Research Management and Governance Team are the main users but we have extended use to the Research Practitioners and Data Managers for study documents and information that is version controlled and in date. All research facing staff involved in study set-up use Edge as part of the process to review capacity and capability. We have plans to extend to clinicians acting as Chief and Principal Investigators. We extended the system for all aspects of Sponsorship, as part of site oversight and management. We also use Edge as part of ECMC reporting and as our Document Control system.
cons: We would like to see a simple way to work on finances and costing templates.

review_title: There's a reason most NHS organisations use EDGE for research data
description: We use EDGE for capturing trial participant recruitment, study management and governance, workflows, finance recording and tracking. calendar sharing and reporting of all study data. Haven't found another system which covers all the required functions as well as EDGE does. The EDGE team are superb - always available for queries, friendly and prepared to help, and there's a great community of EDGE users, happy to share their knowledge and experience.
author: Mat D.
pros: High level of reliability and security. My users have very few complaints and require only a small amount of training to be able to use the system - always a good sign. The facilitation of collaboration with our LCRN and other NHS organisations is brilliant.
cons: Haven't been many changes or features added in the time I have been using EDGE - I'm really looking forward to EDGE 3!

review_title: Evolutionary Development leads to Global Empowerment
description: Unlike many applications EDGE has evolved by the direct involvement of the users it is not designed with any organisation in mind but in a far more democratic manner. As a software package for research it is not perfect but for the customer service and high security storage costs afforded alone it has more than the Edge on its competitors. I doubt there is a similar package out there commercially that would provide the functionality and support of EDGE at the same price.
author: Steven B.
pros: A highly recommend clinical trials management database that is extremely quick and easy to use, and it gives you a noticeably clear overview of trials engagement. One of the few systems that spans the UK and research globally connecting the top professionals within the research community with the same goal. A product that organisations can scale to suit their needs benefitting from direct development involvement
cons: It is difficult to criticise a system I have been extremely supportive of, however there a few suggestions I had made previously that may make there way to EDGE 3, these include automatic appointments and more report filtering capabilities.

description: I have used EDGE for over eight years and couldn't imagine using anything else. The brilliant support team are always on hand to answer any questions you have. Use EDGE - why wouldn't you!
author: Cathy F.
pros: It is so easy to use and everything is in one place when setting up a research study - from the initial idea/concept of a study through to archiving it. You can upload any document you need into the relevant section of the system including sponsor and site information. When you upload a new version of a document, the old version is automatically archived so you don't lose any previous documents - they are all safely hidden away for you. You can keep information private or public depending on what level you require people to view your study. Information is easily found and reports can be run for nearly everything - if it's in EDGE you can get it out in a report! You can create your own fields such as attributes and workflows (checklists) to make the system your own. If you have an idea to improvement EDGE, the Local Administrator can put it onto IdeaScale - you can help develop EDGE for the future.
cons: Unfortunately you can't drag and drop documents into EDGE yet but fingers crossed that will be coming in the next version. You can't amend documents in EDGE as you have to download them and upload the amended version. For me there aren't really any cons as I've already mentioned, you can make your instance your own.

author: Paloma A.
pros: EDGE system is very intuitive and user friendly. It allows our research teams to capture most of their research activity. The new Knowledge-Base with the inclusion of quick guides for all the topics with real examples and tips has got a very positive acceptance among our users. Another function we use and that has made our life easier is sharing bespoken reports with our teams using the 'Report sharing' functionality.
cons: These are some of the enhancements our users would like to see in EDGE. - It would be nice to have folders or a search option in Workflows and Global workflows. At the moment is quite unorganized. - Also to enhance the 'Patient Status menu' allowing double consent for these type of studies. - The audit reports are difficult to understand and track. Additionally, search functionality instead of scrolling through pages to find a user. - More roles and rights to users would be beneficial in a big organization like ours. We hope some of these suggestions could be developed in EDGE 3.

description: I have been using EDGE for nearly two years now, and can't fault it. Its a great system to use to get an oversight of your trial data and to have it all in one place.
author: Becky T.
pros: Very easy and flexible system to use Lets you adapt the system for the needs of your trust to capture data by using attributes, which allows you to run reports on anything you need to. Creating workflows to standardise processes We also use EDGE as document storage for our Clinical Trials.
cons: Drag and drop function for uploading documents would be useful ! apart from that, no cons

description: Helps me do what I need to do
author: Eddie S.
pros: Ease of use & flexibility.
cons: Would like to move to the next version soon.

review_title: EDGE - Easy to use, does what it says on the tin
description: Great system - easy to use, easy to train others to use, now currently could not do my job without it. I am evangelical about EDGE - it's a great design!
author: Claire W.
pros: Easy to use, easy to navigate around interface, tabs do what you think they will, icons are sensible, system does not respond in unexpected ways. Operations are 'foolproof' - it is almost impossible to accidentally delete or overwrite something, which makes you confident to experiment. Support materials are easily accessible and clear, support team are great and really helpful. System can be optimised to have additional 'personalised' functions via Forms and Workflows so can be easily adapted to your own ways of working.
cons: System logs you out if no keystrokes/mouse movements after a very short period of time (however this is required by security rules in NHS so not much they can do about this)

description: Edge is user friendly for all users whether it is used for study set up like me or the logging of new participants to a study or a new researcher who needs to understand the process of submitting a new study. I enjoy training new staff on Edge and showing other members of the team features that we currently do not use within our Trust. If I had it my way we would be using Edge to its full advantage and Edge would be the first port of call for everyone within the department.
author: Alice G.
pros: I use Edge constantly throughout my working day, it is easy to navigate around and keeps track of all the studies that I currently have in set up. I use the reports regularly to keep up to date with the tasks I need to complete each week. Uploading documents has now become so much easier with Edge 3 and there are constant updates to make our work each day easier
cons: Edge logs you out of your account after not using it for 20 mins or so so constantly logging back in.

review_title: Manage all projects in one space
description: I love how Edge can be customized to fit what I need it to do. I can also leave out/ignore features/fields I don't want to use, without any issues.
author: Annemie S.
pros: This software is designed from the site user's perspective and not from the 'top down' like most other software. As a research site, we have visibility of all our projects and participants in one space, regardless of who the study sponsor is or where the study is being conducted. We can easily see the status of all our projects.
cons: There are many ways (almost too many) to navigate the system, which can make it hard for new users to learn/grasp. There is a navigation bar horizontally across the top of the page and another vertically down the left side of the page. This can be confusing for new users.

review_title: Enhancing Research Management with EDGE: Strengths, Challenges, and Potential
description: My overall experience with EDGE has been positive, as it has significantly improved the way we manage research processes, track studies, and maintain data quality. The system streamlines governance, finance, and reporting, making it an essential tool for our team. While it has its limitations—such as the lack of interactive reporting—ongoing improvements and our ability to refine workflows within EDGE continue to enhance its functionality. With the right updates, it has the potential to become an even more powerful research management tool.
author: Victoria D.
pros: I like the ability to streamline research processes, improve our data accuracy, and enhance collaboration across teams. EDGE reduces reliance on spreadsheets, making finance tracking and study management more transparent and efficient. EDGE provides valuable insights through KPIs, making it easier to monitor progress and drive continuous improvements.
cons: The lack of interactive reporting, being able to click into reports and invoices and edit it them from a report, limits our ability to optimise record data quality and work more efficiently in real time. If this feature were available, we would use it extensively to enhance data management, streamline processes, and further improve accuracy.

description: It has been an absolutely amazing experience working with EDGE, and the passionate team who always go above and beyond to make a difference. Keep up the great work!
author: Shazma B.
pros: EDGE provides great visibility and transparency of clinical research landscape in real-time through its exceptional user-friendly interface for capturing, tracking, reporting valuable information, and great team support that make things happen for everyone involved.
cons: EDGE offers a unique collaborative environment that promotes active learning, sharing, and innovating together. This is not only empowering but also, rewarding due to its positive impact on global community. It would be extremely helpful to see more options (potentially in pipeline) around adding forms to bulk participants, view and report costs that are not yet added, breaking down the project recruitment by each financial year within single report, sharing of finance reports with relevant users, and dashboard for a quick snapshot on key performance indicators and for data quality management purposes.

review_title: Get EDGE if you haven't already!
description: EDGE has been my departments life saver, it has improved and aligned nearly all our processes. Could not recommend it enough
author: Ellis B.
pros: EDGE is designed fully to support our Research processes, by people who understand the research processes and participate in them themselves. Its finance tracking tool is a life savour and the access to information is so intuitive and easy to use.
cons: That not everyone uses it! Everyone doing clinical trials in the UK a t least should be using EDGE as their primary system. It helps us all connect with each other!

review_title: EDGE is adaptable to your needs
description: I could not be as efficient or as effective in my job without EDGE. It holds all the information personnel need to know about a trial and it is accessible to all those that need it. I can run reports from EGDE that are built for our needs at service level, it gives us oversight of our trials.
author: Vicky J.
pros: Accessible anywhere, flexible, adaptable to your service, give researcher autonomy in how they run their trials. I love the workflows as I can track processes, add in details so I know exactly where we are at. EDGE is also allowing us to move more from paper to electronic for documenting training and delegation logs, which makes it quicker to sign up personnel to work on trials.
cons: I do not see any cons of using EDGE. For some it is another system they have to log into, which may be a down side, but for me it is an essential system. It would be great if systems could talk to each other more.

review_title: If it's clinical trials related, it's in Edge!
description: We have been using EDGE for 7-8 years and it has been instrumental to our department's management. Prior to EDGE, we did not have an accurate way of tracking our studies and recruitment data. After rolling out EDGE, we have become extremely precise. We use EDGE to track all recruitment activities, assign studies, analyze staff workload, share recruitment reports and trial listings, track ethics submissions and approvals, upload regulatory documents and centralized files, and track basic finance information, among many other uses. Regarding our users, our frontline staff are responsible for entering recruitment information and maintaining patient statuses. We also give EDGE access to our investigators so they can look up protocols and regulatory documents, as well as our active and upcoming trials by disease site. We share reports with all of our users using the My Reports feature for quick retrieval of data. Sharing this data in EDGE has resulted in a great uptake of the system across all types of users (coordinators, CRAs, primary care teams, physicians/investigators, admin assistants, senior management, finance, pharmacy, etc.). The reports we are able to pull from EDGE are second to none - we can build any report, any time, anywhere! Recently, we began using Power BI to create dynamic reports from the data pulled from EDGE to enhance our ability to drill down and ask questions of our data. As we continue to use EDGE, the system truly grows and develops with us.
author: Bianca B.
pros: -Easy to use -Reliable -Fully customizable fields allows you to create meaningful reports -Able to maintain an accurate record of all trials and patients -Having EDGE at your fingertips means that the information you are looking for is always available when you need it -Acts as a repository for all of our protocols and regulatory documents -Users are able to suggest new features or improvements to help build the system for all users. -Networking and sharing ideas with other sites as part of a community of EDGE users. Not only are we able to connect directly with other sites about EDGE, but we can also reach out to other sites about our shared trials. EDGE has truly broken down the "silo-ed" nature of clinical trials.
cons: -Needs a data tracking tool to allow end users and admin to track their patient visits, data entry, and data timeliness. -Would like to see a more robust and automated finance module that integrates with patient visits and data entry. Currently the finance module is very manual. -Enhancements to the user interface would be helpful (e.g. drag-and-drop) (to be addressed in Edge v3)

description: I have used EDGE daily for almost 5 years and it has made my job much easier. It enables us to easily record information about the projects reviewed by our Research and Development team and track the journey of a clinical studies in a clear interface. It was my job to teach our local users how to use EDGE and the its customisable features made it simpler to explain it. The list of cons are really just suggestions on how EDGE could improve in my opinion, not really things I dislike about it. In fact, colleagues who know me well can attest to the fact that I'm pretty effusive about it and could talk about EDGE for hours!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: - User friendly interface - Very customisable (we can add our own fields to suit our needs) - Easy to retrieve information and create bespoke reports - Assigning people to projects is simple and efficient - Enables efficient collaboration between teams and organisations involved in research - A good document storage alternative - A fantastic team behind and responsive support
cons: - More features should be customisable (such as the 'Add a Patient' form) - Mass updating fields would have a been a time-saving feature - More options for account types (e.g. to differentiate between admins and users who need overall 'manager' access, but who should not be able to customise fields)

review_title: Easy to access system with adaptable functionality
description: EDGE is a good company who we have had positive interactions with. When escalation is required to EDGE they are quick to get back to you and offer support via regional contacts if required. It is a very effective research management system.
author: Alessia D.
pros: The system is user-friendly and can allow training to be rolled out with minimal support. There is functionality to use adapt the system within the limits of the programme to be bespoke for your organisation. Allows collaboration, and visibility of data to a wide audience and it is particularly useful to be able to gain access from a web-browser for home working. It has been essential for our switch to home-working during the COVID-19 pandemic.
cons: Creating reports being limited to admin users is not very user friendly due to time constraints for the admin staff. We do not use EDGE for our reporting purposes for that reason, however the ability to create reports for staff to use does get around this slightly.

description: EDGE is helping us to better keep track of our staff training and compliance, as well as governance requirements such as tracking amendment progress and regulatory approvals. As a Sponsor we are using the system to enhance our oversight of sponsored studies and also to provide our researchers with a resource that will improve their clinical research experience.
author: Suzy W.
pros: The flexibility in being able to build your own reports and workflows, whilst also having the facilities to keep track of important elements such as costings, participant safety and staff training requirements. The support of the EDGE team is also fantastic, which enhances the overall experience and ease of using the system.
cons: The inability to give users from one organisation access to another - a lot of our students run their own studies and work at a hospital during this time, so will often have multiple log-ins. I appreciate that this is not a feature due to data protection requirements, so we work around this. More often than not the students don't mind because they have access to a system that will make managing their study less burdensome!

description: Very proactive team who are always moving forward with the functionality of Edge. The helpdesk is always available and ready to answer any questions or offer help and advise on functionality.
author: Julie D.
pros: I have used Edge for over 10 years, initially Version 1 followed by Version 2. The team at Edge are always looking to improve the functionality of the system and also regularly look at suggestions from users on their 'Got an Idea' board. The system is easy to use, and teams can get as much out of it as they put in. Using entities and attributes enable additional information to be captured that may be relevant locally, and workflows can monitor the various processes and timescales. Being a web based system allows access across the site.
cons: We have had to adapt and use some Edge functionality in a different way for the Primary Care setting as the amount of data used is a lot larger than that in our Secondary Care.

description: Very good The Edge team are also motivated to improve the database further.
author: Will R.
pros: Easy for the 200+ research delivery staff at the hospital to use. Does not take a long time to train a new member of staff. Lots of additional features for administrators and clinical users. All of the data stored in Edge has a good level of data protection and is very easy to report on. Glitches in Edge are very rare and the Edge team are very quick to help with any queries or problems.
cons: Some areas of the database require a few updates. What is most highly anticipated at St George's with the release of Edge 3 is updated calendar so that CRF manager can be replaced, automated emails, live link with Microsoft Power BI and drag and drop capability when uploading files.
